Examples

Sample Snippet

RasterDataset

The RasterDataset associated with this raster band.

virtual IRasterDataset RasterDataset { get; }

Returns

IRasterDataset

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>RasterDataset</STRONG> object represents a dataset on disk or in a database. It is composed of one or more persistent raster bands. The dataset provides access only to operations that apply to the entire dataset, with the <STRONG>RasterBand</STRONG> providing access to operations that occur on individual bands.</P> <P>The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and <STRONG>Raster</STRONG> objects representing other aspects of the data. It can be used to examine dataset properties including different format, extent, spatial reference, and number of bands.</P> <P>The spatial reference of the raster raster dataset can be changed through interface <STRONG>IGeoDatasetSchemaEdit</STRONG>, but it does not project the dataset, only changes the coordinate system associated with the dataset.<BR>The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.</P>

RasterInfo

The raster information.

virtual IRasterInfo RasterInfo { get; }

Returns

IRasterInfo

Returns

Examples

Sample Snippet

Workspace

The workspace containing this dataset.

virtual IWorkspace Workspace { get; }

Returns

IWorkspace

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<p>A Workspace is a container of spatial and non-spatial datasets such as feature classes, raster datasets, and tables. It provides methods to instantiate existing datasets and to create new datasets. Workspaces are classified into types specified by the <b><a href="esriWorkspaceType.htm">esriWorkspaceType</a></b> enumerator: FileSystemWorkspace, LocalDatabaseWorkspace, and RemoteDatabaseWorkspace. Shapefiles and ArcGIS for Desktop Advanced workspaces are examples of FileSystemWorkspace. A personal geodatabase stored in an Access or a File Geodatabase is an example of a LocalDatabaseWorkspace. A geodatabase stored in an RDBMS such as Oracle, DB2, SQL Server, or Informix and accessed via ArcSDE is an example of a RemoteDatabaseWorkspace.</p>&nbsp;<p>A Workspace hands out a <b><a href="WorkspaceName.htm">WorkspaceName</a>&nbsp;</b>object as the value of its FullName property. For example, the WorkspaceName for a workspace can be persisted in a map document. An application can call the <b>Open()</b> method on the workspace name after loading it from persistent storage to connect to and get an object reference to the workspace.</p>

Write(IPnt, IPixelBlock) 

virtual void Write(IPnt tlc, IPixelBlock pxls)

pxls IPixelBlock
